text: Does tool granularity change the TYPE of agent failure, not just the failure rate? On puzzles where toolsets fail, does the dominant failure mode differ across none/fine/medium/coarse (e.g. budget-exhaustion vs wrong-submit vs stuck-loop vs blind tool-trust)?

comments.feasibility: Not answerable on current data (checked 2026-06-11): of 39 failed trials only 13 are genuine reasoning failures - the other 26 are rate-limit/harness aborts (exit-code-1, 0-1 tool calls). The 13 give no structured signal (0 wrong-submit, 0 budget-exhaustion; all 'early-stop'/'gave-up'), and the discriminating modes (stuck-loop, blind tool-trust, bookkeeping-drown) require reading the trajectories. To answer it needs: (a) a clean batch of genuine non-aborted failures, n>=5 per toolset, on a puzzle where all toolsets fail (puzzle_003/004); (b) an LLM trajectory classifier labelling each failed log's failure mode; then test whether the mode distribution differs by granularity (chi-square / its small-n exact analog).
